quick_facts.label: Role scope
quick_facts.value: A Ticketing and Reservations Executive handles travel bookings and related customer service across transport and accommodation products; in India, official tourism material describes travel-agency services as covering ticketing, itineraries, transport and accommodation.

quick_facts.label: Relevant national skill benchmarks
quick_facts.value: Comparable Indian skill qualifications include Airline Reservation Agent (AAS/Q0302), listed at NSQF Level 4, and Ticketing Consultant (THC/Q4302), listed at NSQF Level 5.

quick_facts.label: Degree linkage
quick_facts.value: Tourism-and-travel degrees are directly relevant: Ministry of Tourism travel-agent guidelines specify that a qualified employee may hold a Diploma or Degree in Tourism and Travel Management from a recognised university, IITTM or an AICTE-approved institution.

quick_facts.label: Core systems exposure
quick_facts.value: Reservation work is system-led. IGNOU's tourism-business operations curriculum explicitly covers Centralised Reservation Systems (CRS) and Global Distribution Systems (GDS).

quick_facts.label: Training context
quick_facts.value: IITTM's tourism-and-travel syllabus treats airline ticketing, fare calculation and GDS familiarity as essential entry-level capabilities for airline and tour-operation jobs, with practical exposure to Amadeus commands and itinerary interpretation.

sections.heading: What the role does
paragraphs: This is a customer-facing travel-operations role: the executive turns a traveller's requirements into accurate reservations, confirms and updates itineraries, and coordinates ticketing or other booked services. The exact product mix differs by employer—such as air, rail, surface transport, hotel or packaged-travel bookings—but official Ministry of Tourism material identifies ticketing, itineraries, transport and accommodation among core travel-agency services.

A strong operational emphasis is accuracy: booking details, fare or service rules, travel documents and customer communications must be handled correctly. This is especially relevant where reservations are managed through digital platforms, CRS/GDS tools or supplier booking systems.
bullets: Create, amend, cancel and reconfirm reservations and communicate booking details to customers.
Build and interpret travel itineraries; coordinate tickets with transport, accommodation and other travel services.
Use reservation and distribution systems while applying applicable booking, fare, supplier and travel-documentation requirements.
Handle enquiries and service recovery professionally when schedules, availability or customer requirements change.

sections.heading: Entry routes after BTTM
paragraphs: A Bachelor of Tourism and Travel Management is a well-aligned immediate parent qualification because it provides tourism-sector knowledge and a credible route into travel agencies, tour operations and transport-related work. For example, IGNOU identifies travel agency, tour operation, accommodation and transportation among the employment areas associated with its bachelor-level tourism and travel programme.

To become job-ready for ticketing and reservations, supplement the degree with hands-on practice in CRS/GDS workflows, itinerary construction, fare and rule interpretation, and booking amendments. IITTM's tourism-and-travel curriculum specifically frames airline ticketing, fare calculation and GDS familiarity as essential for entry-level airline and tour-operation roles.

A skills-first route can also be relevant, particularly for airline-reservation work: AASSC lists Airline Reservation Agent as a 540-hour Level 4 course, while NSDC lists the associated AAS/Q0302 qualification at Level 4. Candidates should verify the current status, provider quality, assessment arrangements and employer recognition of any course before enrolling.
bullets: BTTM or a comparable tourism/travel degree, followed by practical reservation-system training and an internship or entry-level travel-operations role.
A recognised tourism or aviation skill qualification for an entry route, then employer-specific system and product training.
Customer-service experience combined with demonstrable booking-system, itinerary and documentation knowledge.

sections.heading: Skills to develop
paragraphs: The role combines travel-domain knowledge with digital operations and service communication. Formal tourism curricula cover CRS and GDS tools, while IITTM's ticketing syllabus includes itinerary interpretation, aviation geography and codes, time calculations, fares and rules, and Amadeus commands. Ministry of Tourism guidance also highlights effective communication skills and knowledge of foreign languages in the travel-agent context.

Employers may use different systems and sell different combinations of travel products, so competence should be demonstrated through accurate end-to-end transactions rather than by a software name alone.
bullets: CRS/GDS and supplier booking-system literacy; reservation creation, amendment, cancellation and confirmation.
Itinerary reading and construction; airport/city/airline codes, time zones and travel-product knowledge.
Fare, rule and policy interpretation; careful data entry and reconciliation of traveller details.
Clear written and spoken customer communication; calm handling of changes, complaints and time-sensitive requests.
Working knowledge of travel documentation and applicable travel requirements; additional languages can be an advantage.

sections.heading: Where the work is found
paragraphs: Ticketing and reservation functions can sit in travel agencies, tour operators, airline or aviation-service environments, hospitality reservation desks and corporate/public-sector travel-management operations. This breadth is reflected in official sources: IGNOU identifies travel agencies, hotels, tour operators and transportation as tourism-career settings; AASSC maintains a dedicated Airline Reservation Agent course; and India Tourism Development Corporation's Ashok Travels and Tours provides air-ticketing and broader travel services.

The work may be office-, contact-centre- or digitally delivered, with peak workloads around service disruptions, seasonal demand and customer travel dates. The employer determines shift patterns, sales targets, transaction authority and the mix of domestic versus international bookings.
bullets: Corporate, government or public-sector travel-management providers.

sections.heading: Possible progression
paragraphs: Progression usually comes through broader product knowledge, stronger transaction accuracy, client handling and responsibility for more complex itineraries or a team. NSDC's tourism-and-hospitality listings show a useful skills-framework ladder around the role: Reservation Desk Executive is listed at Level 4 in the retired standards catalogue, while Ticketing Consultant is listed at Level 5 and Team Leader–Travel at Level 5 (with a separate Team Leader listing at Level 6). These are related occupational benchmarks, not a guarantee of a particular employer's title or promotion sequence.

Possible next specialisms include airline ticketing, travel consulting, visa assistance, travel insurance, tour operations, corporate travel or reservations supervision. Candidates should compare actual job descriptions, as title usage varies widely across Indian employers.
bullets: Reservations/ticketing executive to specialist or consultant-level ticketing work.
Travel consultant, visa-assistance, insurance or tour-operations specialisation.
Senior executive, team-lead or travel-desk supervisory responsibility after proven operational and customer-service performance.
